name: Atomic parity violation: weak charge of cesium
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the measured weak charge Q_W(133Cs) of cesium, the most precise low-energy test of the electroweak theory available outside collider experiments. Combined experimental and atomic-theory result Q_W(Cs) = -73.71 +/- 0.35 (Wood et al. + atomic-structure calculations) agrees with SM prediction Q_W^SM(Cs) = -73.23 +/- 0.01.

regime.description: Atomic-scale physics with weak-neutral-current contribution. Sensitivity to TeV-scale Z' bosons and to electroweak loop corrections.

current_bound.experiment: Boulder cesium APV (Wood et al. 1997) plus atomic-structure calculations (Dzuba et al., Porsev et al.)
current_bound.reference: Wood et al., 'Measurement of Parity Nonconservation and an Anomalous Anapole Moment in Cesium', Science 275, 1759 (1997); Dzuba, Berengut, Flambaum, Roberts, PRL 109, 203003 (2012), arXiv:1207.5864

what_it_excludes: Constrains Z'-mediated contributions to electron-quark neutral currents at the TeV scale. Sensitive to the running of the weak mixing angle from M_Z down to atomic scales (large logarithm). Currently most precise low-energy probe of electroweak couplings outside the Z resonance.

parameterization.name: SMEFT semileptonic operators O_lq^(1), O_lu, O_ld, O_eq, O_eu, O_ed in their parity-violating combinations.
parameterization.reference: Erler and Su, Prog. Part. Nucl. Phys. 71, 119 (2013), arXiv:1303.5522.
parameterization.summary: Q_W = -2[Z(C_1u + 2 C_1d) + N(2 C_1u + C_1d)] in SM; SMEFT contributions appear as shifts in C_1u, C_1d.

procedural.predicted_observable: Q_W(Cs) extracted from parity-violating optical rotation in atomic transitions, divided by atomic-physics matrix elements computed by relativistic many-body theory.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Constrains specific SMEFT Wilson-coefficient combinations at |C / Lambda^2| <~ (5 TeV)^-2.
framework_inputs: Match framework onto SMEFT and read off C_1u, C_1d (electron-up and electron-down parity-violating couplings) at the atomic scale.
Sensitivity to running of sin^2(theta_W) from M_Z down to ~MeV scales.

primary_references.citation: Wood et al., 'Measurement of Parity Nonconservation and an Anomalous Anapole Moment in Cesium', Science 275, 1759 (1997)
Dzuba, Berengut, Flambaum, Roberts, 'Revisiting parity nonconservation in cesium', PRL 109, 203003 (2012)
Erler and Su, 'The Weak Neutral Current', Prog. Part. Nucl. Phys. 71, 119 (2013)
